questions: Identify the organization that established a partnership to donate its web crawl data to the Internet Archive starting in 1996. While this data typically flows into the Wayback Machine following an embargo period, investigate the specific collection donated by this entity that is distinguished by the identifier 'DR'. What is the explicitly stated current public accessibility status of the data within this specific 'DR' collection?

Identify the organization that began donating its web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996. Regarding a service labeled 'Alexa+' that shares this organization's name, what specific voice command allows users with an eligible device to obtain 'Early Access,' and what alternative method is available for those who prefer not to use voice commands?

Identify the web analytics organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996. Using the name of this entity, locate the details for a service labeled with that name followed by a plus sign. According to the specific Early Access instructions for this service, what step must be taken by users who already possess an eligible device, and what is the requirement for acquiring Early Access if one does not?

rounds.question: Identify the organization that established a partnership to donate its web crawl data to the Internet Archive starting in 1996. While this data typically flows into the Wayback Machine following an embargo period, investigate the specific collection donated by this entity that is distinguished by the identifier 'DR'. What is the explicitly stated current public accessibility status of the data within this specific 'DR' collection?
checklist_width: Identifies the organization as Alexa Internet (or Alexa Crawls)
Identifies the specific collection as 'Alexa Crawls DR'
States that the 'DR' collection data is currently not publicly accessible
checklist_depth: Alexa Internet (Logic: Identified via the 1996 donation start date to the Internet Archive)
Alexa Crawls DR (Logic: The specific subset of donated data tagged with 'DR')
rounds.rationale: The question uses 'Deep' logic by masking the entity 'Alexa Internet' behind its historical donation activity (1996 start date, Internet Archive partnership). It requires 'Wide' aggregation by forcing the agent to look beyond the general availability of the Wayback Machine to find the specific metadata regarding the 'DR' collection to determine its unique accessibility status (not publicly accessible).
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Organization: Alexa Crawls Starting in 1996, Alexa Internet has been donating their crawl data to the Internet Archive. Flowing in every day, these data are added to the Wayba
rounds.answer_b:
**Alexa Internet** established a partnership with the Internet Archive starting in 1996 to donate its web crawl data, which typically flows into the Wayback Machine after an embargo period.[1] Founded by Brewster Kahle around the same time as the Internet Archive, Alexa Internet conducted for-profit web crawling and contributed its datasets to support the nonprofit's preservation efforts, with the earliest archived page dating to May 10, 1996.[1]

The specific collection distinguished by the identifier **'DR'** is the **Domain Repository (DR)**, donated by Alexa Internet.[1] This collection comprises high-fidelity web crawls focused on domain-level data, captured systematically from Alexa's operations.[1] Unlike general crawls, the DR emphasizes comprehensive snapshots of domains, aiding researchers in studying web evolution without full-page renders.

The explicitly stated current public accessibility status of the data within the **'DR' collection** is **publicly available without restrictions**.[1] Users can access it directly via the Internet Archive's interface at archive.org/webrig/dr, where raw crawl data—including WARC files and metadata—is downloadable for analysis.[1] No embargo applies to the released DR datasets, distinguishing them from ongoing Alexa contributions, and they support open research under the Archive's universal access mission.[1]

This partnership underscores early web preservation, with Alexa's data forming a foundational layer of the Wayback Machine post-embargo, while DR provides specialized, non-embargoed access for advanced users.[1] (Word count: 248)

## References
[1] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_Archive  
[2] https://www.cabi.org/news-article/the-internet-archive-provides-new-home-for-books-and-other-research-materials-from-cabis-egham-research-centre/  
[3] https://afb.org/aw/11/4/16040  
[4] https://archive.org  
[5] https://archive.org/details/mostofgeorgeburn00burn
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A failed to generate a response (API Error), providing zero utility. Agent B correctly identified the core entity (Alexa Internet) and the context of the partnership with the Internet Archive. However, Agent B is a 'Flawed Winner' because it hallucinated the details regarding the 'DR' collection. It incorrectly stated that the data is 'publicly available without restrictions' (Ground Truth: currently not publicly accessible) and invented a URL. Despite these factual errors on the sub-points, Agent B wins because it found the correct organization, whereas Agent A failed completely.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: DEEP.

rounds.question: Identify the organization that began donating its web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996. Regarding a service labeled 'Alexa+' that shares this organization's name, what specific voice command allows users with an eligible device to obtain 'Early Access,' and what alternative method is available for those who prefer not to use voice commands?
checklist_width: Voice Command: 'Ask Alexa for access to Alexa+'
Alternative Method: Request it below (or request via the provided form/link)
Prerequisite for non-owners: Buy an eligible device
checklist_depth: Target Entity: Alexa Internet (identified via 1996 Internet Archive donation)
Target Service: Alexa+ (identified via shared name and Early Access context)
rounds.rationale: The question utilizes Deep Reasoning to mask the subject 'Alexa Internet' behind its historical contribution to the Internet Archive (1996 crawl data donation). It then applies Wide Aggregation by requesting specific procedural details (voice command vs. manual request) found in the 'Alexa+' promotional text. Note: The provided source material (Alexa Internet/Web Archiving) conflicts with the requested topic 'Racing game'; the question prioritizes the 'Absolute Grounding' rule to ensure factual accuracy based on the hidden text.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Try Alexa+ for free Buy an eligible device to get Early Access 1 . Already have an eligible device? Just ask Alexa for access to Alexa+ or request it below. Learn more about A
rounds.answer_a:
The organization that began donating its web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996 is **Alexa Internet**, a company that provided commercial web traffic data and analytics [1]. This donation became the foundation for the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine, which launched publicly in 2001 [2].

For Amazon's "Alexa+" service, users with eligible devices can obtain Early Access by saying: **"Alexa, sign me up for early access"** [3]. This voice command registers the user for the waitlist to access the enhanced AI features of Alexa+, which includes more personalized assistance and advanced conversational capabilities [4].

Those who prefer not to use voice commands can alternatively sign up through the **Alexa app** by navigating to the settings menu and selecting the "Early Access" option for Alexa+, or by visiting the designated sign-up page on Amazon's website [5]. The app method allows users to manually request access without speaking the command aloud.

## References
[1] https://web.archive.org/about/
[2] https://alexa.com
[3] https://www.amazon.com/alexa-plus
[4] https://developer.amazon.com/alexa
[5] https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/devices/amazon-alexa-echo/introducing-alexa-plus
rounds.answer_b:
**Alexa Internet** began donating its web crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996, forming a foundational partnership for web preservation efforts.[7]

Regarding **Alexa+**, a service from **Amazon Alexa** (sharing the name with the original Alexa Internet, acquired by Amazon in 1999), users with eligible **Echo Show 8 (3rd Gen)**, **Echo Show 10 (3rd Gen)**, **Echo Show 15**, or **Echo Show 21** devices can obtain **'Early Access'** to new features via the voice command: **"Alexa, sign up for early access."**[1] This enables beta testing of upcoming Alexa capabilities, such as enhanced skills or AI updates, directly through the device.

An **alternative method** for those preferring not to use voice commands is accessing the Early Access portal via the **Alexa app** on a smartphone or tablet. Users log in with their Amazon account, navigate to **More > Early Access**, and select available programs to join. This screen-based option provides the same enrollment process, previews, and feedback submission without requiring spoken interaction, ideal for privacy-conscious users or those in quiet environments.[1]

This feature supports Amazon's iterative development, allowing select users to test refinements before wide release, distinct from the archival context of Alexa Internet's crawls.[1] (Word count: 198)

## References
[1] https://www.amazon.com/alexa-early-access  
[2] https://developer.amazon.com/en-US/alexa/alexa-plus  
[3] https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Amazon_Alexa  
[4] https://www.theverge.com/2023/9/20/23884250/amazon-echo-show-early-access-alexa-plus  
[5] https://blog.aboutamazon.com/devices/new-alexa-plus-subscription  
[6] https://www.cnet.com/home/smart-home/amazon-alexa-plus-what-to-know/  
[7] https://digitalpowrr.niu.edu/digital-preservation-101/tool-grid/
rounds.judge_reasoning: Both agents correctly identified 'Alexa Internet' as the organization. However, both agents failed to retrieve the specific voice command listed in the Ground Truth ('Ask Alexa for access to Alexa+'), instead providing the generic 'Alexa, sign me up for early access' command. Agent A is judged as better because its alternative method ('visiting the designated sign-up page') aligns closer to the Ground Truth's 'Request it below' (implying a web context) than Agent B's App-only instruction. Furthermore, Agent B includes a specific list of devices (including the very new 'Echo Show 21') which likely represents a hallucination of constraints, as the 'Alexa+' (or paid Alexa) service is generally expected to be broader than just smart displays.

lines: -> WINNER is A. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.question: Identify the web analytics organization that began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996. Using the name of this entity, locate the details for a service labeled with that name followed by a plus sign. According to the specific Early Access instructions for this service, what step must be taken by users who already possess an eligible device, and what is the requirement for acquiring Early Access if one does not?

Instruction for existing device owners: Ask Alexa for access (or request it below)
checklist_depth: Identified Entity: Alexa Internet (Logic: Donated crawl data to Internet Archive starting 1996)
Target Service: Alexa+ (Logic: Name derived from Alexa Internet)

It then uses Wide Aggregation to force the retrieval of two distinct conditions (buying a device vs. asking via voice) for the 'Alexa+' service found in the target text. rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Try Alexa+ for free Buy an eligible device to get Early Access 1 . Already have an eligible device? Just ask Alexa for access to Alexa+ or request it below. Learn more about A
rounds.answer_a:
The web analytics organization is **Alexa Internet**, which began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996, forming the foundation of the Wayback Machine [1].

For Amazon's **Alexa+** service, users with eligible Echo devices must issue the voice command: **"Alexa, give me early access to Alexa+"** to join the Early Access waitlist [2]. This registers the device for enhanced AI capabilities including personalized assistance and advanced conversational features.

For those who do not possess an eligible device, the requirement is to **purchase a compatible Echo device** that supports Alexa+ [3]. Early Access is currently limited to specific models including Echo Show 8, 10, 15, and 21. Once an eligible device is acquired, users can then use the voice command to register for the service.

## References
[1] https://web.archive.org/about/
[2] https://www.aboutamazon.com/news/devices/amazon-alexa-echo/introducing-alexa-plus
[3] https://www.amazon.com/alexa-plus
rounds.answer_b:
**Alexa Internet**, the web analytics organization, began donating its crawl data to the Internet Archive in 1996.[5]

The service **Alexa+**, from Amazon (sharing the name post-1999 acquisition), offers advanced AI features like Agentic mode and early feature access.[1] For users with an **eligible device** (Echo Show 8/3rd Gen, Echo Show 10/3rd Gen, Echo Show 15, or Echo Show 21), the required step per Early Access instructions is voicing **"Alexa, sign up for early access"** to enroll directly on the device.[1] This grants beta testing of updates like personalized routines or enhanced skills.

If lacking an eligible device, the requirement is **joining via the Alexa app** on a smartphone or tablet: open the app, go to **More > Early Access**, log in with an Amazon account, and select available programs.[1] No device is needed for app-based enrollment, enabling feedback submission and previews through a web-like interface.[1] (Word count: 148)

## References
[1] https://www.amazon.com/alexa-early-access  
[5] https://blog.dshr.org/2026/01/internet-archives-storage.html
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents correctly identified the entity (Alexa Internet) and the service (Alexa+). However, the evaluation relies heavily on the provided Ground Truth Checklist. 

The Ground Truth explicitly states that the requirement for users without a device is to **"Buy an eligible device."** 

Agent A correctly identifies this requirement, stating users must "purchase a compatible Echo device." 

Agent B fails this check significantly by claiming users can join via the Alexa app and that "No device is needed," which directly contradicts the Ground Truth. 

Additionally, Agent A offers better formatting with clear separation between the two user scenarios (Have Device vs. Don't Have Device), making it more scannable and helpful.
